Since Kunda longitude can be in Moon Nakshatra or its
trines, these mathematical points in the wheel ofd estiny or
births could have a difference of 120/81 = 1° -28' -53.33".
That is if we know the birth time with an accuracy of at least
3 minutes, then this method can be used to rectify and derive
the correct BT.
The length ofa Nakshatra is equivalent to a day. Or in
other words, it is equal to the 360 degree rotation of Lagna.
That is, in a day, by the time Moon takes to cover 13° 20'
there is only 81 x 3 = 243 moments in the wheel of destiny at
which birth could take place (for a specific location).
Practically, within this period lakhs ofb irths could take place
all around the world. (Approximately, two lacs children take
birth every day all around the world). In the period of a
